steps.title: Use a scale shortcut for s
steps.content: The $x$-values are $-2s$, $-s$, and $s$. Because all $x$-values are scaled by the same nonzero constant $s$, set $s=1$ to see the pattern quickly. The representative points are $(-2,24)$, $(-1,21)$, and $(1,15)$.

steps.title: Translate the fitted line back to s
steps.content: With $s=1$, Desmos gives $y=-3x+18$. Restoring the original scale means the slope is $-\frac{3}{s}$, so $$y=-\frac{3}{s}x+18$$ Multiply by $s$: $$sy=-3x+18s$$ Then rearrange: $$3x+sy=18s$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is $3x+sy=18s$, so B is correct.
